issues: [gem route balance] Large direct resource region [10,1]->[11,3] has resource_weight=6.0, resources=4, gems=2: redGem@[11,1], redPotion@[11,2], blueGem@[10,3], pickaxe@[11,3].

summary: current: MT0 uses valid 13x13 tiles, allowed enemies, exact MT0 resource quotas, no adjacent enemies, viable key-door ordering, and three plausible entrance-to-exit route choices. Budget delta is within remaining whole-tower limits. | connectivity density: Connectivity and density pass: wall_ratio 0.657 and monster_density_non_wall 0.259 fit a compact high-pressure MT0 style. The graph has a controlled central route plus left and right gated side routes; main_cycle_rank_ratio 0.121 does not indicate uncontrolled open-grid sprawl. min_route_to_exit has 3 enemies and 2 yellow doors, enough baseline pressure for this floor role. | resource balance: Resource access is adequately staged: the entrance/free region exposes no resources or tools, low_cost_resource_clusters is empty, keys are distributed across central/left/right route stages, the blue door at [9,6] opens a stronger top-right shortcut/reward pocket, and the pickaxe at [11,3] is protected by door and blackSlime pressure. | monster damage: Monster pacing is acceptable for MT0: the minimum route uses a low-tax greenSlime at [6,10], mid redSlime at [6,7], and threshold blackSlime at [6,4] with a redPotion compensation at [6,6]. Side branches add distinct bat/redSlime/blackSlime costs around protected rewards. No zone or repulse sources are placed, so special-damage review is not applicable. | gem route balance: Gem route balance reviewer found 1 route/resource imbalance issue(s).
